title: Example Use of MixedUpSlimSims
text: See package README configuring system able run . main issue define MUP_CONDA path R option system (Unix/Linux) environment variable. Note done code since Rmarkdown seems read .Rprofile. can get started.
code: library(reticulate) #> Warning: package 'reticulate' was built under R version 4.4.3 library(tidyverse) #> Warning: package 'ggplot2' was built under R version 4.4.3 library(MixedUpSlimSims) library(CKMRpop)  # this is necessary to use the MixedUpSlimSims::pairwise_relationship()                   # until I fix how data get loaded in CKMRpop.  # The user should set the value of MUP_CONDA to point to the conda # environment with all the things needed. options(MUP_CONDA = "/Users/eriq/miniforge3/envs/mixed-up-parents") Sys.setenv(MUP_CONDA = "/Users/eriq/miniforge3/envs/mixed-up-parents")   # set paths for python within R reticulate::use_condaenv(condaenv = getOption("MUP_CONDA"))   # also set the path for system calls here Sys.setenv(PATH = paste0(getOption("MUP_CONDA"), "/bin:", Sys.getenv("PATH")))

title: Example Use of MixedUpSlimSims
text: going use allele frequencies estimated Cyclone Creek. put vignettes/inputs. WCT B RBT. , simulations, population 1 like WCT getting steady flow RBT population 2 like RBT just RBT continually coming WCT habitat. , want 1 B 2. Now need get form need simulate_founder_genos(). give markers cumulative positions, meaning position total genome (just within chromosome). function now, ! must write input file founder genotypes slim_input.vcf. , simulate 1000 indivs population write genotypes .
code: cc_freqs <- read_csv("inputs/cyclone-creek-variable-markers-freqs.csv") #> Rows: 628 Columns: 4 #> ── Column specification ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Delimiter: "," #> chr (1): chrom_f #> dbl (3): pos, A, B #>  #> ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data. #> ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message. head(cc_freqs) #> # A tibble: 6 × 4 #>   chrom_f      pos        A       B #>   <chr>      <dbl>    <dbl>   <dbl> #> 1 omy01    5604542 1.00     0.0400  #> 2 omy01   15520536 0.502    0.00309 #> 3 omy01   16635910 0.493    0.367   #> 4 omy01   17490399 0.932    0.00329 #> 5 omy01   17490429 0.0657   0.00239 #> 6 omy01   18387369 0.000522 0.323 cc_freqs2 <- cc_freqs %>%   mutate(chrom = as.character(chrom_f)) %>%   select(-chrom_f) %>%   cumulative_genome_position(mykiss_chroms)   frqs <- cc_freqs2 %>%   rename(af1 = A, af2 = B, POS = cPOS) %>%   select(POS, af1, af2) simulate_founder_genos(frqs, N = c(1000, 1000), pop_names = c("wct", "rbt")) %>%   write_input_vcf(out_path = "slim_input.vcf")

title: Example Use of MixedUpSlimSims
text: can just run SLiM . run current directory . use system call, (though Rmarkdown run shell block). SLiM file look like: Now can run SLiM writes lot vcf files genotypes simulated individuals: also writes file called SLiM.trees.
code: # get the SLiM file.  It ships with the R package. file <- system.file("SLiM-models/2-pop-10-gens-vcf-initialize.slim", package = "MixedUpSlimSims") initialize() {     defineConstant("n_p1", 1000); // number of individuals in the RBT pop     defineConstant("n_p2", 1000); // number of individuals in the WCT pop      initializeSLiMOptions(keepPedigrees=T); // turn on pedigree tracking; see section 25 of the manual (25.7, pg. 679)     defineConstant("L", 1949962539); // length of genome     initializeTreeSeq();     initializeMutationRate(0); // zero mutations forward-in-time; will be added by msprime afterwards     initializeMutationType("m1", 0.5, "f", 0.0); // neutral mutations     m1.convertToSubstitution = F; // keep loci in the simulation, even if they reach fixation     initializeGenomicElementType("g1", m1, 1.0);     initializeGenomicElement(g1, 0, L);       // set up 29 trout chromosomes with not much recombination within them     ends = c(84884017, 84884018, 170364868, 170364869, 255302337, 255302338, 340358758, 340358759, 432561311, 432561312, 515492034, 515492035, 595255810, 595255811, 679034094, 679034095, 747501830, 747501831, 818558021, 818558022, 898836325, 898836326, 988491333, 988491334, 1054543576, 1054543577, 1134902301, 1134902302, 1198270468, 1198270469, 1269166547, 1269166548, 1345694384, 1345694385, 1407413604, 1407413605, 1466989977, 1466989978, 1508401989, 1508401990, 1560331576, 1560331577, 1608881719, 1608881720, 1657923568, 1657923569, 1698286047, 1698286048, 1780887703, 1780887704, 1821070223, 1821070224, 1866387099, 1866387100, 1907331003, 1907331004, 1949962539);     rates = c(1e-09, 0.5, 1e-09, 0.5, 1e-09, 0.5, 1e-09, 0.5, 1e-09, 0.5, 1e-09, 0.5, 1e-09, 0.5, 1e-09, 0.5, 1e-09, 0.5, 1e-09, 0.5, 1e-09, 0.5, 1e-09, 0.5, 1e-09, 0.5, 1e-09, 0.5, 1e-09, 0.5, 1e-09, 0.5, 1e-09, 0.5, 1e-09, 0.5, 1e-09, 0.5, 1e-09, 0.5, 1e-09, 0.5, 1e-09, 0.5, 1e-09, 0.5, 1e-09, 0.5, 1e-09, 0.5, 1e-09, 0.5, 1e-09, 0.5, 1e-09, 0.5, 1e-09);     initializeRecombinationRate(rates, ends);     initializeSex("A"); //Make all chromosomes autosomal } 1 late() {     //make two pops, with ongoing gene flow until the end of the simulation     sim.addSubpop("p1", n_p1); // rainbow trout population     sim.addSubpop("p2", n_p2); // westslope population      // Read in a .vcf that was made in R and has p1 indivs first and p2 indivs second     c(p1,p2).genomes.readFromVCF("slim_input.vcf", m1);       sim.treeSeqRememberIndividuals(sim.subpopulations.individuals); // retains first-gen p1 and p2 individuals to determine genomic region population-of-origin;                                                                     // automatically recorded by default, but doesn't hurt to keep explicitly     p1.setMigrationRates(p2, 0.05); // migration rate from WCT into RBT     p2.setMigrationRates(p1, 0.01); // migration rate from RBT into WCT }   2: late() {   // remember individuals over all generations so we can plot them and see how   // the admixture is proceeding   sim.treeSeqRememberIndividuals(sim.subpopulations.individuals); } 9:11 late() {  // Last three years write everyone out to a separate VCF file named pX-T.vcf                // where X is the subpop.id and T is the cycle number   for (subpop in sim.subpopulations) {     subpop.outputVCFSample(subpop.individualCount, replace=F, filePath="p" + subpop.id + "-" + sim.cycle + ".vcf");   } }  11 late() {     sim.treeSeqOutput("SLiM.trees"); //  write out trees } # run it via system system(paste("slim -s 333 ", file, collapse = " ")) dir(pattern = "p[0-9]+-[0-9]+\\.vcf") #> [1] "p1-10.vcf" "p1-11.vcf" "p1-9.vcf"  "p2-10.vcf" "p2-11.vcf" "p2-9.vcf"

title: Example Use of MixedUpSlimSims
text: 11 years simulation, want get things tree-seq years 0 10 populations 1 2. like : Now read simplified tree py$ts object, can turn tibbles nodes indivs easily: Now get vector founder nodes. ones existed time 10 (counting backwards 0) TS time, time 1 SLiM time. Now want get focal nodes. just individuals final three generations, want plot pictures admixture process action, going make focal individuals everyone SLiM year 2 forward (, TS time 9 forward 0), pops 1 2. designate desired years pops tibble. population labels come SLiM put tskit point base-1 indexed, named 1 2, 0 1. can use like : Note fnl focal nodes divided different years, following function cycles years finds ancestors separately, otherwise samples become linked ancestors (link_ancestors tskit), thus excluding actual founders (couldn’t use tspop directly—seem handle multiple cohorts samples) absolutely need . Now get ancestral segments .
code: # read the trees. Dump just captures the python code that was # evaluated to get things in the py variable in the global env. dump <- read_and_filter_trees(   trees_path = "SLiM.trees",   years_list = list(`1` = 0:10, `2` = 0:10) ) ni <- ts_nodes_and_inds(py$ts) founders <- founder_node_ids_and_pops(ni, 10) focal_tibble <- tibble(   pop = rep(c(1,2), each = 10),   time = rep(0:9, 2) )  focal_tibble #> # A tibble: 20 × 2 #>      pop  time #>    <dbl> <int> #>  1     1     0 #>  2     1     1 #>  3     1     2 #>  4     1     3 #>  5     1     4 #>  6     1     5 #>  7     1     6 #>  8     1     7 #>  9     1     8 #> 10     1     9 #> 11     2     0 #> 12     2     1 #> 13     2     2 #> 14     2     3 #> 15     2     4 #> 16     2     5 #> 17     2     6 #> 18     2     7 #> 19     2     8 #> 20     2     9 fnl <- get_focal_nodes(   ni,    Focal = focal_tibble ) segments_tib <- ancestral_segs(   ts = py$ts,   focal_nodes_list = fnl,   founder_nodes = founders$nodes,   nodes_tib = ni$nodes_tib,   indiv_tib = ni$indiv_tib )

what: Obtaining and plotting the ancestry tracts within each individual
title: Example Use of MixedUpSlimSims
text: function : can plot like :  left column population 1 WCT pop . right column population 2 RBT. colors denote different ancestry combinations, coded decimal equivalents trits ternary numbers. 2 = 2 doses p1; 4 = 1 dose p1 one p2; 6 two doses p2. can extend two species. Perhaps explanation needed trits. envision number doses population ternary number. ternary number, digit can 0, 1, 2. 1’s column (303^0), 0, 1 2, signify just —0, 1, 2. 3’s column, values 0=00=0, 1=31=3 2=62=6. forth (just like binary, base 3). Since counting doses sum digits must two. examples three-species case: 002: two copies species 1 — 32×0+31×0+30×2=23^2 \times 0 + 3^1 \times 0 + 3^0 \times 2 = 2 011: one copy species 1 one copy species 2 — 32×0+31×1+30×1=43^2 \times 0 + 3^1 \times 1 + 3^0 \times 1 = 4 020: two copies species 2 — 32×0+31×2+30×0=63^2 \times 0 + 3^1 \times 2 + 3^0 \times 0 = 6 200: two copies species 3 — 32×2+31×0+30×0=183^2 \times 2 + 3^1 \times 0 + 3^0 \times 0 = 18 Coding things way make easy extend even species future, needed.

what: Finding pairwise relationships amongst the sampled individuals
title: Example Use of MixedUpSlimSims
text: go assess well parentage can done cases, curious see often aunts uncles end called parents, etc. order make sort tally, need know actual relationships sampled individuals. pedigree connects , can use find relationships. employ functions CKMRpop package Eric wrote. Though keep wrapped function. can use find relatives amongst 6000 individuals last 3 generations like : can really quickly count relationships tallied step: Key relationsihps: “avuncular”, meaning aunt-niece uncle-niece, etc. FC first cousin GP grandparent-grandchild `PO1 parent-offspring Si sibling max_hit column records many ancestors shared individuals level relationship. Si max_hit = 1 half-sibling, Si max_hit = 2 full-sibling. Looking , clear want also use modified SLiM script fish totally random mating—enough full-siblings , compared half siblings. (Hence MixedUpParents paper also used non-Wright-Fisher model SLiM produced many full-siblings).
code: pairwise_relats <- pairwise_relationships(   IT = ni$indiv_tib,   sample_years = 0:2,   num_gen = 2 ) pairwise_relats %>%   count(dom_relat, max_hit) #> # A tibble: 10 × 3 #>    dom_relat max_hit     n #>    <chr>       <int> <int> #>  1 A               1 32160 #>  2 A               2    97 #>  3 FC              1 47910 #>  4 FC              2   363 #>  5 FC              3     1 #>  6 GP              1  7982 #>  7 GP              2     9 #>  8 PO              1  8000 #>  9 Si              1 12140 #> 10 Si              2    14

title: Example Use of MixedUpSlimSims
text: can simulate diagnostic markers individuals last three years pretty easily. cyclone creek diagnostic markers available package data MixedUpSlimSims. place individuals last three years.
code: sim_diag <- example_spp_diag_markers %>%   cumulative_genome_position(mykiss_chroms) %>%   rename(diag_spp_idx = diag_spp) %>%   place_diagnostic_markers(IndivSegs = indiv_segs %>% filter(ind_time %in% 0:2)) #> Running bedtools in /var/folders/ln/5brnn1wx0vj58fhlbbdjm8h40000gp/T//RtmpjFMwSA/file117f36009ab6a #> Rows: 7782000 Columns: 5 #> ── Column specification ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Delimiter: "\t" #> chr (1): chrom #> dbl (4): diag_spp_idx, pos, ped_id, geno #>  #> ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data. #> ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.

what: Writing out those two data sets
title: Example Use of MixedUpSlimSims
text: also want store information individuals—basically metadata pedigree, true Q-values. stored Q_values variable. also want keep actual segments fish genotypes . wanted write two marker data sets along “truth,” :
code: set.seed(1)  subsamp <- sample(unique(sim_diag$ped_id), size = 2500, replace = FALSE)  sim_diag_ready <- sim_diag %>%   filter(ped_id %in% subsamp) %>%   rename(diag_spp = diag_spp_idx) %>%   mutate(diag_spp = case_match(diag_spp, 1 ~ "WCT", 2 ~ "RBT", 3 ~ "YCT")) %>%   rename(n = geno, indiv = ped_id) %>%   mutate(indiv = as.character(indiv), pos = as.integer(pos), n = as.integer(n))  # the variable markers are a little more work because we have to get back to the # original mykiss genome positions cc_freqs3 <- cc_freqs2 %>%   select(pos, chrom, cPOS)  sim_var_snps_ready <- sample_genos_long %>%   filter(ped_id %in% subsamp) %>%   select(pos, ped_id, haplo, allele)  %>%   rename(cPOS = pos) %>%   left_join(cc_freqs3, by = join_by(cPOS)) %>%   select(chrom, pos, ped_id, allele) %>%   group_by(chrom, pos, ped_id) %>%   summarise(n = sum(as.integer(allele))) %>%   ungroup() %>%   arrange(ped_id, chrom, pos) %>%   rename(indiv = ped_id) %>%   mutate(indiv = as.character(indiv), pos = as.integer(pos)) #> `summarise()` has grouped output by 'chrom', 'pos'. You can override using the #> `.groups` argument. Q_values #> # A tibble: 40,000 × 9 #>    ind_id anc_pop admix_fract ind_pop ind_time   sex ped_id ped_p1 ped_p2 #>     <dbl>   <dbl>       <dbl>   <int>    <dbl> <int>  <int>  <int>  <int> #>  1      0       1     0             1        0     0  20000  19195  19946 #>  2      0       2     1             1        0     0  20000  19195  19946 #>  3      1       1     0             1        0     0  20001  19433  19577 #>  4      1       2     1             1        0     0  20001  19433  19577 #>  5      2       1     0.00533       1        0     0  20002  19425  19804 #>  6      2       2     0.995         1        0     0  20002  19425  19804 #>  7      3       1     0             1        0     0  20003  19195  19668 #>  8      3       2     1             1        0     0  20003  19195  19668 #>  9      4       1     0.0182        1        0     0  20004  19435  19912 #> 10      4       2     0.982         1        0     0  20004  19435  19912 #> # ℹ 39,990 more rows  true_segs <- indiv_segs %>%   filter(ped_id %in% subsamp) dir.create("results/004", showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE) sim_results <- list(   spp_diag = sim_diag_ready,   var_snps = sim_var_snps_ready,   metaQ = Q_values,   true_segs = true_segs,   cumul_genome = mykiss_chroms,   alle_freqs = cc_freqs ) write_rds(sim_results, file = "results/004/sim_results.rds", compress = "xz")

what: R Utilities for Running SLiM and Processing tskit Trees
title: R Utilities for Running SLiM and Processing tskit Trees
text: MixedUpSlimSims R package facilitate simulations admixed populations using SLiM, particularly collect results terms ancestry tracts within simulated individuals way tskit. depends lot non-R dependencies (like python, tskit, etc.) uses reticulate hood work set dependencies.

title: R Utilities for Running SLiM and Processing tskit Trees
text: can install development version MixedUpSlimSims GitHub : order use , also set conda environment. Install conda environment includes slim, tskit, numpy, pandas, bedtools bcftools. version work, SLiM version 4. can name environment anything want, easiest name mixed--parents. SEDNA: working SEDNA cluster, installed conda environment, additional step keep pandas failing looking version GLIBCXX exist system: unless get errors look like: ImportError: /lib64/libstdc++..6: version 'GLIBCXX_3.4.29' found Update ~/.bashrc (whatever file use initialize shell) export path conda environment just installed environment variable MUP_CONDA. ~/.bashrc includes lines: : change /Users/eriq/miniforge/envs/mixed--parents whatever path mixed--parents conda environment. using cluster, activate conda environment entering R. general, working package, issue commands beginning script make sure R know find things (changing paths appropriate):
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("eriqande/MixedUpSlimSims") conda create -n mixed-up-parents -c conda-forge -c bioconda \     anaconda::pandas \     conda-forge::numpy \     conda-forge::slim==4.2.2 \     conda-forge::tskit  \     bedtools \     bcftools conda activate mixed-up-parents  # activate the environment pip install pandas --no-cache-dir --force-reinstall  # reinstall pandas in there # For MixedUpSlimSims export MUP_CONDA=/Users/eriq/miniforge/envs/mixed-up-parents options(MUP_CONDA = "/Users/eriq/miniforge3/envs/mixed-up-parents") Sys.setenv(MUP_CONDA = "/Users/eriq/miniforge3/envs/mixed-up-parents")  # set paths for python within R reticulate::use_condaenv(condaenv = getOption("MUP_CONDA"))  # also set the path for system calls here Sys.setenv(PATH = paste0(getOption("MUP_CONDA"), "/bin:", Sys.getenv("PATH")))

text: Markers tibble must columns: diag_spp_idx, gives 1-based index species 1 allele fixed marker (species fixed 0 allele). cPOS, positions cumulative base pairs SLiM uses. chrom: name chromosome (actual genome modeled) pos: position base pairs chromosome chrom. row tibble must specify unique species-diagnostic marker, species-diagnostic markers wish place simulated individuals included tibble. IndivSegs tibble individual segments like returned function indiv_ancestry_tracts(). Though can arbitrary columns, must include least following: ped_id: pedigree id individual left: left endpoint interval right: right endpoint interval anc1: 1-based index ancestry haplotype 1 within interval. anc2: 1-based index ancestry haplotype 2 within interval.

title: Place diagnostic marker alleles within individauls according to their ancestry haplotypes — place_diagnostic_markers
code: # Prepare Markers. Name the columns right, and get cumulative cPOS in there. M <- example_spp_diag_markers %>%   dplyr::filter(diag_spp < 3) %>%   dplyr::rename(diag_spp_idx = diag_spp) %>%   cumulative_genome_position(Cumul = mykiss_chroms)  # Now, also get some example indiv-segs. We have some as a package # data object. IS <- example_indiv_segs  # Then run the function: place_diagnostic_markers(M, IS) #> Running bedtools in /var/folders/ln/5brnn1wx0vj58fhlbbdjm8h40000gp/T//RtmpaRz4gx/file116fa5e002ffd #> Rows: 10580 Columns: 5 #> ── Column specification ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Delimiter: "\t" #> chr (1): chrom #> dbl (4): diag_spp_idx, pos, ped_id, geno #>  #> ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data. #> ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message. #> # A tibble: 10,580 × 5 #>    diag_spp_idx chrom      pos ped_id  geno #>           <dbl> <chr>    <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl> #>  1            1 omy01  5604476  20055     2 #>  2            2 omy01  5604516  20055     0 #>  3            1 omy01  5604556  20055     2 #>  4            2 omy01  8362156  20055     0 #>  5            2 omy01  9582183  20055     0 #>  6            1 omy01 12303335  20055     2 #>  7            2 omy01 16142369  20055     0 #>  8            2 omy01 16142395  20055     0 #>  9            2 omy01 16206842  20055     0 #> 10            2 omy01 16206875  20055     0 #> # ℹ 10,570 more rows

title: Simulate a complete data set to run in MixedUpParents — slim_sim_a_dataset
code: slim_sim_a_dataset(   AF,   genome_info,   founder_pop_nums,   slim_file,   years_list,   marker_pop_time_list,   diagnostic_markers,   slim_seed = sample(1:1e+06, 1),   focal_nodes = NULL,   num_gen = 2 )

title: Simulate a complete data set to run in MixedUpParents — slim_sim_a_dataset
text: AF tibble allele frequencies columns must chrom pos, many columns wish, one holding frequency 1 allele population.  names columns give names species/subspecies. genome_info tibble cumulative base pairs different chromosomes genome giving positions AF.  necessary easily translating positions continuous coordinates SLiM.  Must columns chrom, start, end, cumul_start, cumul_end.  See mykiss_chroms example. founder_pop_nums vector number diploids founder population.  commensurate beginning population size SLiM simulation file. slim_file relative absolute path .slim file used SLiM simulation. must written creates: VCF output format "p" + subpop.id + "-" + sim.cycle + ".vcf" tree sequence recorded output named "SLiM.trees" years_list named list. name integer signifying SLiM population, value integer vector tree-seq years individuals/nodes tree seq named population retained tree-sequence. See documentation read_and_filter_trees() information.  must concordant SLiM file used. marker_pop_time_list list pops years extract genotypes VCF files.  named list names names population "p1", "p2", etc. years SLiM years (get higher later time) samples desired.  example: list(p1 = 9:11, p2 = 9:11). diagnostic_markers tibble columns diag_spp, chrom, pos. values diag_spp must include species codes used column headers AF. slim_seed single integer seed use SLiM.  Gets set automatically R RNG set. focal_nodes tibble specifying populations (base-1 indexed) cohorts (base-0 tskit time—recent 0) want determine ancestry vectors.  left NULL done populations non-founder generations. num_gen number generations back trace relationships individuals.  default 2, get parent-offspring, full- half siblings, grandparent/grandchild, aunts uncles first cousins.

title: Simulate a complete data set to run in MixedUpParents — slim_sim_a_dataset
text: Note also currently configured infers founder generation' (tskit time) time smallest ped_id existed.

title: Simulate a complete data set to run in MixedUpParents — slim_sim_a_dataset
code: AF <- cyclone_creek_var_freqs genome_info <- mykiss_chroms founder_pop_nums <- c(1000, 1000) slim_file <- system.file(   "SLiM-models/2-pop-10-gens-vcf-initialize.slim",   package = "MixedUpSlimSims" ) slim_seed <- 55 years_list <- list(`1` = 0:10, `2` = 0:10) marker_pop_time_list <- list(p1 = 9:11, p2 = 9:11) diagnostic_markers <- wct_rbt_yct_diagnostic_markers  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # this takes more time than CRAN likes, so don't run it on Check simmed <- slim_sim_a_dataset(   AF = AF,   genome_info = genome_info,   founder_pop_nums = founder_pop_nums,   slim_file = slim_file,   slim_seed = slim_seed,   years_list = years_list,   marker_pop_time_list = marker_pop_time_list,   diagnostic_markers = diagnostic_markers )  } # }

text: simulated SLIM dataset slim_sim_a_dataset(), can use function following: Downsample consistent subsample number NV variable markers /number ND diagnostic ones. Create missing data remaining markers simple rate MV MD across variable diagnostic markers, respectively. Randomly toss individual sample sample single rate S across individuals, pops times. Downsample number individuals specific number fraction population time (yet implemented).

title: Subsample SNPs or individuals and simulated genotyping error — tweak_slim_simmed_data
code: tweak_slim_simmed_data(   X,   NV = NA,   ND = NA,   S = NA,   MV = NA,   MD = NA,   EV = NA,   ED = NA )

title: Subsample SNPs or individuals and simulated genotyping error — tweak_slim_simmed_data
text: X return object slim_sim_a_dataset(). NV number variable markers randomly downsample , retaining markers across individuals completely discarding rest. NV greater total number variable markers warning issued full set markers returned. ND number diagnostic markers randomly downsample , retaining markers across individuals completely discarding rest. ND greater total number diagnostic markers warning issued full set markers returned. S fraction sampled individuals randomly retained.  , want sampled 40% population set S = 0.4. MV missing data data rate variable markers. genotype variable-marker data set (subsampling NV markers) independent probability MV missing applied. MD missing data data rate diagnostic markers. genotype variable-marker data set (subsampling NV markers) independent probability MV missing applied. EV probability non-missing variable marker genotype individual genotyping error.  genotype simulated genotyping error, true genotype 1 choose either 0 1, probability 1/2. true genotype 0 2, genotyping error results 1.  (Genotyping error opposite homozygote occur). ED probability non-missing diagnostic marker genotypes individual genotyping error.  genotype simulated genotyping error, true genotype 1 choose either 0 1, probability 1/2. true genotype 0 2, genotyping error results 1.  (Genotyping error opposite homozygote occur).

title: Subsample SNPs or individuals and simulated genotyping error — tweak_slim_simmed_data
text: returns list downsampled genotyping-errored versions data set.
